title: Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance Gaps In Low- And Middle-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring antimicrobial resistance surveillance gaps in LMICs, linking WHO GLASS data, national laboratory data, and antibiotic-consumption data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: AMR caused 1.27 million deaths directly and contributed to 4.95 million deaths in 2019, with the highest burden in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. WHO GLASS collects AMR surveillance data from 127 countries but sub-national coverage is sparse and laboratory capacity is concentrated in capital cities. Linking GLASS reporting to laboratory-capacity maps identifies where AMR burden is highest but surveillance is weakest.

success_metrics.target: Every surveillance-gap estimate links GLASS, laboratory, and consumption data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
